---
name: avm-tf-documentation
description: Use this skill whenever an Azure Verified Module (AVM) for Terraform needs documentation work — writing or editing `_header.md` (the README content above the auto-generated section), `_footer.md` (the README content below, including the mandatory Data Collection notice), regenerating `README.md` via `terraform-docs` (`./avm docs` / `make docs`), or writing the per-example `_header.md` / `_footer.md` files. The headline rule is NEVER edit README.md directly — it is auto-generated from _header.md + the Terraform sources + _footer.md and your changes will be overwritten on the next `./avm docs` run. Also covers `.terraform-docs.yml` configuration and what makes a Registry-friendly README. Trigger on phrases like "AVM README", "_header.md", "_footer.md", "terraform-docs AVM", "make docs", "./avm docs", "update README", "README out of date", "documentation drift", "auto-generated README", "Registry README".
---

# AVM Terraform documentation

The headline rule: **NEVER edit `README.md` directly.** It is auto-generated by `terraform-docs` from `_header.md` + the Terraform sources (variables, outputs, providers, resources) + `_footer.md`. Editing `README.md` will lose your changes on the next `./avm docs` (or `make docs`) run, and `./avm pr-check` will fail because the README is out of date with the sources.

Authoritative sources:
- [TFNFR23](https://azure.github.io/Azure-Verified-Modules/spec/TFNFR23) — `_header.md` / `_footer.md`
- [SFR3](https://azure.github.io/Azure-Verified-Modules/spec/SFR3) — Data Collection notice (in `_footer.md`)
- `terraform-docs` docs: <https://terraform-docs.io/>
- The template's `_header.md` and `_footer.md` at <https://github.com/Azure/terraform-azurerm-avm-template>

## How the README is assembled

```
README.md  =  _header.md
            + (auto-generated table-of-contents)
            + (auto-generated "Requirements" — from terraform.tf)
            + (auto-generated "Providers" — from terraform.tf)
            + (auto-generated "Modules" — from any `module "..." {}` calls)
            + (auto-generated "Resources" — from every resource and data source)
            + (auto-generated "Required Inputs" — every variable with no default)
            + (auto-generated "Optional Inputs" — every variable with a default)
            + (auto-generated "Outputs")
            + _footer.md
```

`.terraform-docs.yml` at the repo root controls the formatting; it's identical across AVM TF modules and shouldn't need editing.

## `_header.md` — what you write

This is the content **above** the auto-generated section. The convention is:

```markdown
<!-- BEGIN_TF_DOCS -->
<!-- markdownlint-disable MD041 -->
# terraform-azurerm-avm-res-search-searchservice

This is the [AVM](https://aka.ms/AVM) resource module for the Azure AI Search service.

## Features

- AzAPI-first implementation of `Microsoft.Search/searchServices`
- Standard AVM cross-cutting interfaces:
  - Diagnostic settings
  - Role assignments
  - Locks
  - Private endpoints
  - Customer-managed keys
  - System / user-assigned managed identities
- WAF-aligned defaults: local API keys disabled, AAD-only auth, public network access off when private endpoints are configured

## Quick start

```hcl
module "search" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-search-searchservice/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.1"

  name                = "srch-myapp-prod-001"
  location            = "westeurope"
  resource_group_name = "rg-myapp-prod"
}
```

See `examples/` for more usage scenarios.

## Limitations

- Currently does not deploy data-plane resources (indexes, indexers, data sources). Use the module's outputs to drive a separate AzAPI / data-plane Terraform configuration.
```

### Required sections in `_header.md`

- A title (the repo name).
- A one-paragraph description.
- A short "Features" list — what does this module add over a raw `azapi_resource`?
- A "Quick start" code block showing minimal usage.
- Any "Limitations" or "Known issues" the consumer should see before scrolling.

Don't duplicate things that will be auto-generated below (full input table, full resource list, etc.).

### `_header.md` must NOT include

- The "Requirements" / "Providers" / "Resources" / "Inputs" / "Outputs" tables — those are auto-generated.
- Marketing language about Azure or AVM at length — keep it brief; the consumer can read the AVM home page if they want context.

## `_footer.md` — what goes below

```markdown
## Data Collection

The software may collect information about you and your use of the software and send it to Microsoft. Microsoft may use this information to provide services and improve our products and services. You may turn off the telemetry as described in the [repository](https://aka.ms/avm/telemetry). There are also some features in the software that may enable you and Microsoft to collect data from users of your applications. If you use these features, you must comply with applicable law, including providing appropriate notices to users of your applications together with a copy of Microsoft's privacy statement. Our privacy statement is located at <https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkID=824704>. You can learn more about data collection and use in the help documentation and our privacy statement. Your use of the software operates as your consent to these practices.
<!-- END_TF_DOCS -->
```

The Data Collection notice is the only **mandatory** content in `_footer.md` (per SFR3) and the template ships with it pre-populated. Don't remove it. You can add a "Contributing" or "Support" section after it if it helps consumers, but most AVM modules just leave the footer as-is.

## Example READMEs

**Every `examples/<name>/` directory has its own `_header.md` and `_footer.md`**, and its own auto-generated `README.md`. The header should explain what the example demonstrates:

```markdown
<!-- examples/private-endpoint/_header.md -->
# Private endpoint example

This example shows how to deploy the Search service module with a private endpoint, including:

- A virtual network and subnet for the endpoint
- A Private DNS Zone for `privatelink.search.windows.net`
- Disabled public network access on the Search service
```

The `examples/.terraform-docs.yml` controls per-example doc generation; it's also boilerplate and shouldn't need editing.

## When does the README get regenerated?

- Manually: `./avm docs` or `make docs`.
- As part of: `./avm pre-commit` (which runs `./avm docs` and fails if the working tree is dirty afterwards).
- As part of: `./avm pr-check` and CI.

If CI says "README is out of date", it means you committed code changes without regenerating the README. Run `./avm pre-commit` and commit the resulting `README.md` diff.

## What if `terraform-docs` isn't reading my variable descriptions correctly?

- Make sure descriptions are one-line OR use a heredoc (`<<DESCRIPTION ... DESCRIPTION`). Mixing styles confuses the parser.
- Variables with object types should describe each field of the object inside the heredoc so consumers see what shape to pass.
- For sensitive outputs, set `sensitive = true` — `terraform-docs` shows `<sensitive>` in the output table, which is more useful than the actual value would be.

## Registry-friendly README

Once `0.1.0` is tagged, the README lands on the Terraform Registry at `registry.terraform.io/modules/Azure/avm-res-<rp>-<type>/azurerm`. The Registry parses the Markdown and shows a "Provisioning Instructions" panel separately, so:

- Keep code blocks tight and minimal — the Registry doesn't fold them.
- Link to `examples/<name>/README.md` (not absolute GitHub URLs) — the Registry rewrites relative links.
- Use unambiguous section headings (`## Features`, `## Quick start`, `## Limitations`) so the Registry's auto-generated TOC reads cleanly.

## Common pitfalls

- **Editing `README.md` directly.** Lost on the next `./avm docs` run. Edit `_header.md` / `_footer.md`.
- **Deleting the Data Collection notice from `_footer.md`.** SFR3 violation; CI fails.
- **Including the input/output tables in `_header.md`.** They'll appear twice in the final README.
- **Not running `./avm docs` after adding a variable.** README will lag the code; `./avm pr-check` will fail.
- **Forgetting per-example `_header.md`.** The example's auto-generated README will have no introduction and consumers won't know what they're looking at.
- **Using absolute GitHub blob URLs in the Markdown.** Relative paths work in both GitHub and the Registry; absolute URLs only work in one.
- **Putting the Data Collection notice anywhere except `_footer.md`.** Anywhere in `README.md` directly = overwritten. Anywhere in `_header.md` = appears before the auto-generated tables, which is jarring.
